zh.Kilmaurs, Ayrshire, Scotland: 蘇格蘭艾斯爾吉爾莫爾斯
zh.lifeSummary: Margaret Dennieston於1223年出生在蘇格蘭艾斯爾吉爾莫爾斯。她於從1240年到1241年與William de Cunynghame, of Kilmaurs在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1285年在她的家鄉享年62歲。

zh-hans.Kilmaurs, Ayrshire, Scotland: 苏格兰艾斯爾吉爾莫爾斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Margaret Dennieston于1223年出生在苏格兰艾斯爾吉爾莫爾斯。她于从1240年到1241年与William de Cunynghame, of Kilmaurs在苏格兰结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1285年在她的家乡享年62岁。

nameMeanings.name: Dennieston
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from the barony of Danzielstoun in Renfrewshire, named with the personal name Daniel (for the phonetic significance of the -z-, see Dalzell ) + Middle English toun, tone ‘settlement’ (Old English tūn).

variants.name: Deniston
meanings: English: variant of Denston, a habitational name from either Denstone in Staffordshire (named from the Old English personal name Deni + tūn ‘estate, farmstead’ meaning ‘farmstead of a man called Deni’), or from Denston in Suffolk (from the Old English personal name Deneheard + tūn ‘farmstead of a man called Deneheard’).

variants.name: Daniel
meanings: English, French, Spanish, Portuguese, German, Polish, Czech, Slovak, Romanian, Jewish, Assyrian/Chaldean, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and African (mainly Nigeria and Tanzania); Breton (rarely Le Daniel); Hungarian (Dániel): from the Biblical personal name Daniel, Syriac (Assyrian/Chaldean) Dānīʾēl, Hungarian Dániel (from Hebrew Daniyyel ‘God is my judge’), borne by a major prophet in the Bible. The chief factor influencing the popularity of the personal name (and hence the frequency of the surname) was undoubtedly the dramatic story in the Book of Daniel recounting the prophet's steadfast adherence to his religious faith despite pressure and persecution from the Mesopotamian kings in whose court he served: Nebuchadnezzar, Belshazzar, and Darius. The name was also borne by a 2nd-century Christian martyr and by a 9th-century hermit, the legend of whose lives were popular among Christians during the Middle Ages. Among Orthodox Christians in Eastern Europe the name was also popular as being that of a 4th-century Persian martyr, who was venerated in the Orthodox Church. In France, this surname is most common in Brittany.

Irish (Tipperary and Waterford): shortened form of McDaniel , which is actually a variant of McDonnell , from the Gaelic form of Irish Donal (equivalent to Scottish Donald), erroneously associated with the Biblical personal name Daniel. See also O'Donnell .
Americanized or Germanized form of Slovenian Danijel: from the Biblical personal name Danijel ‘Daniel’ (see 1 above).

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: Margaret Dennieston was born in 1223, in Kilmaurs, Ayrshire, Scotland. She married William de Cunynghame, of Kilmaurs from 1240 to 1241, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1285, in her hometown, at the age of 62.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret Dennieston est née en 1223, à Kilmaurs, Ayrshire, Écosse. Elle a épousé William de Cunynghame, of Kilmaurs du 1240 au 1241, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée en 1285, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 62 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Margaret Dennieston wurde 1223 in Kilmaurs, Ayrshire, Scotland geboren. Sie hat von 1240 bis 1241 in Scotland William de Cunynghame, of Kilmaurs geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1285 im Alter von 62 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Margaret Dennieston è nata il nel 1223 nel Kilmaurs, Ayrshire, Scotland. Lei sposato William de Cunynghame, of Kilmaurs da 1240 a 1241, nel Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1285, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 62 anni.

ja.Kilmaurs, Ayrshire, Scotland: スコットランドエイルシュアイアキルマアウルズ
ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Denniestonは1223年にスコットランドエイルシュアイアキルマアウルズで生まれた。から1240年まで1241年にスコットランドでWilliam de Cunynghame, of Kilmaursと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1285年に故郷で62歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Dennieston(은)는 1223년 스컷랜드 에이르실르 킬모르스에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1240년 부터 1241년 까지 스컷랜드에서 William de Cunynghame, of Kilmaurs(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1285년 그녀의 고향에서 62세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Margaret Dennieston nasceu em 1223, em Kilmaurs, Ayrshire, Scotland. Ela casou-se com William de Cunynghame, of Kilmaurs de 1240 a 1241, em Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 1285, em sua cidade natal, com 62 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret Dennieston родилась в 1223 г. в местности килмаурс, аиршир, Шотландия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William de Cunynghame, of Kilmaurs с 1240 по 1241 в местности Шотландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1285 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 62 лет.

sm.1240 to 1241: mai le 1240 e o'o I le 1241

es.lifeSummary: Margaret Dennieston nació en 1223, en Kilmaurs, Ayrshire, Scotland. Se casó con William de Cunynghame, of Kilmaurs desde 1240 hasta 1241, en Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1285, en su pueblo, a la edad de 62 años.
